channel.title: theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Same-day hospital discharge found to be safe for select patients after transcatheter aortic valve implantation
item.description: With careful selection, same-day hospital discharge was found to be feasible and safe in around one-fifth of patients undergoing transcatheter aortic ...

item.title: Fueling for gold: How nutrition impacts strength, speed, and recovery in Olympic athletes
item.description: If our bodies are machines and food is fuel, it is safe to say that elite performance requires high-octane nutrition. As the Winter Olympics continue ...

item.title: Brain organoids can be trained to solve a goal-directed task
item.description: Imagine balancing a ruler vertically in the palm of your hand: you have to constantly pay attention to the angle of the ruler and make many small adju...

item.title: Targeted radiation therapy improves quality of life outcomes for patients with multiple brain metastases, finds study
item.description: In a Phase III randomized trial, Mass General Brigham researchers found that stereotactic radiation targeting individual tumors led to lower symptom b...

item.title: Red blood cells soak up sugar at high altitude, protecting against diabetes
item.description: Scientists have long known that people living at high altitudes, where oxygen levels are low, have lower rates of diabetes than people living closer t...

item.title: RHR: Vitamin D: Why a Personalized Approach Is Best

item.title: RHR: Cold Exposure: Why Sustained Beats Extreme
item.description: Cold plunges and ice baths dominate social media, but do they really deliver the best metabolic benefits? In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, ...

item.title: RHR: Why Your Sleep Schedule Matters As Much As Duration

item.title: RHR: Menopause Brain Fog—What’s Really Happening (and How To Fix It)
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris examines research confirming that menopause-related brain fog is real, measurable, and reversible. H...

item.title: RHR: Seed Oils and Colon Cancer – What the New Research Really Shows
